description:
You are acting as a build engineer who is preparing a hand-off bundle for the infrastructure team.  
Your goal is to stage a tiny “firewall pack” under /home/user/deploy that contains:

1. A POSIX-shell script named /home/user/deploy/firewall_rules.sh that, when run as root, will configure a minimal IPv4 firewall for the build server.
2. A companion verification file named /home/user/deploy/firewall_rules.log that lists the **exact** commands present in firewall_rules.sh together with a fixed line-number prefix so that automated tests can easily diff the two.

Functional requirements for firewall_rules.sh  
(The script must contain *only* the six commands listed below, in the stated order, one command per line, with no blank lines and no extra whitespace.)

1) iptables -F  
2) iptables -P INPUT DROP  
3) iptables -A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT  
4) iptables -A INPUT -m state --state ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT  
5) iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT  
6) iptables -A INPUT -p tcp -s 127.0.0.1 --dport 8081 -j ACCEPT  

Logging specification  
• /home/user/deploy/firewall_rules.log must contain the same six commands, but each line must be prefixed by a two-digit line number followed by a vertical bar, exactly as shown below.  
• No leading or trailing spaces are allowed on any line.  
• The file must end with a single trailing newline.

Expected format (do **not** include this boxed note inside the file):

01|iptables -F  
02|iptables -P INPUT DROP  
03|iptables -A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT  
04|iptables -A INPUT -m state --state ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT  
05|iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT  
06|iptables -A INPUT -p tcp -s 127.0.0.1 --dport 8081 -j ACCEPT  

Other requirements  
• Create the directory /home/user/deploy if it does not yet exist.  
• Make firewall_rules.sh executable (chmod 750 is sufficient).  
• Do not actually execute the firewall script; you are only staging the files.  
• Feel free to display the two file paths or a success message to stdout, but do not print the file contents themselves.

The automated grader will check:  
a) That /home/user/deploy exists and is a directory.  
b) That /home/user/deploy/firewall_rules.sh exists, is executable, and has the exact six lines in the correct order with no extra whitespace.  
c) That /home/user/deploy/firewall_rules.log exists, is **not** executable, and contains the six numbered lines in the required format.  
d) That each line in both files ends with a Unix newline (LF, 0x0A).  

Accomplish this using normal user privileges only; no sudo is available.
truth:
Directory that must exist at grading time:
  /home/user/deploy  (mode 755 or 750 or 700 is acceptable)

Files that must exist at grading time:

1) /home/user/deploy/firewall_rules.sh  
   • Mode must have execute bit for owner (e.g., 750).  
   • Exact contents (including the terminating newline):

iptables -F
iptables -P INPUT DROP
iptables -A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
iptables -A INPUT -m state --state ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
iptables -A INPUT -p tcp -s 127.0.0.1 --dport 8081 -j ACCEPT

2) /home/user/deploy/firewall_rules.log  
   • Must NOT be executable.  
   • Exact contents (including the terminating newline):

01|iptables -F
02|iptables -P INPUT DROP
03|iptables -A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
04|iptables -A INPUT -m state --state ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
05|iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
06|iptables -A INPUT -p tcp -s 127.0.0.1 --dport 8081 -j ACCEPT

No other files are graded.
